Dear Ms. Wardlowe:
I would fj-rst like to express my sincere aPpreciation for your time and
assistance in talking with me today regardling the Bob Dorr Election
committee and my questions regarding compliance with the Proposition 73
contribution limits during the special Election cycle for state senate
District +1. I believe it is safe to say that there is 
some confusion
surrounding the mechanical aspects.
I r^/ant to reiterate the committee's particular circumstances as well as
set forth my understanding of our conversation. On June 4, 1990, Bob
Dorr fj-1ed form 501, Notice of Intention, for the 1992 election in State
Senate District Number one. Funds vrere raised and expenditures made
during 1990 and the required semi-annual reports were filed in Ju1y,
1990, and January, 1991. Subsequent to the vacating of the District 
1
Senate seat by John Doolittle, 
Bob Dorr has filed an amended Form 501
designating his intent to Participate in the March, L9, L991r Special
Election. 
An amended Form 502 has also been filed redesignating the
Comnittee bank account to be utilized for that Special EIection.
It is my understanding that as of January 2, 1991, the date the seat
was vacated by Mr. Doolittle, 
a Speciat Election cycle began which
constitutes a new fiscal period for purposes of applying the Proposition
73 contribution limitations and that the cycle will end on March 19,
1991-, the date of the election. 
If a run-off election is required,
another fiscal period will begin on March 20, I99L, and will end upon
the date of that run-off election. 
Since the Bob Dorr Cornniltee has
chosen to carry over fund.s on hand at January 2, 1991, for use during

order to determine eligibility 
for future contributions. As you and I
discussed, we propose to make that apportionment on the basis of
assuming funds on hand are attributabfe to the most recent contributions

from pre-January l, 
L989, contributions (Regulation 18535.1) and'
therefore, would be consid.ered reasonable. We also agreed that the

We discussed the inclusion of contributions received subsequent to

bi11s of the Committee existing on January 2, 199L, \,/ou1d need to be

It is my understanding that verbal advice from your office may not be
consid.ered as "formal advice. " I would, therefore, respectfully request
written comrnunication addressing the items discussed in this letter.
Due to the fact that r"re must file our first Pre-Election Statement thi.s
week, I would appreciate a telephone call if I have misstated anything
from our conversation.
assistance.

sections.question_synthetic: The sender requests written confirmation of verbal advice regarding how to apportion carry-over campaign funds ($45,000) from a prior election cycle to determine contributor eligibility under Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election beginning January 2, 1991.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Sender explicitly states verbal advice may not be considered 'formal advice' and requests written communication. No formal Q/C sections present. Document appears to be a follow-up to a phone consultation with FPPC staff member Carla Wardlowe.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender requests written confirmation of verbal advice regarding how to apportion carry-over campaign funds ($45,000) from a prior election cycle to determine contributor eligibility under Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election beginning January 2, 1991.

embedding.summary: This is a letter from a CPA representing the Bob Dorr Election Committee requesting formal written confirmation of verbal advice previously given by FPPC staff regarding the apportionment of carry-over funds and compliance with Proposition 73 contribution limits for a special election in State Senate District 1.
